mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index grows faster than 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations compress real equity valuations and force discount rate repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where the inflation threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 expands.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals abrupt supply-side contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where the production threshold is breached.
